steps.title: Compute the result
steps.content: Simplify: $$f(144) = 4 + 12 = 16.$$ That matches choice B — B is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice B is correct. The value of $f\left(144\right)$ is the value of $f\left(x\right)$ when $x=144$. It's given that the function $f$ is defined by $f\left(x\right)=4+\sqrt{x}$. Substituting $144$ for $x$ in this equation yields $f\left(144\right)=4+\sqrt{144}$. Since the positive square root of $144$ is $12$, it follows that this equation can be rewritten as $f\left(144\right)=4+12$, or $f\left(144\right)=16$. Therefore, the value of $f\left(144\right)$ is $16$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C is incorrect. This is the value of $f\left(1,296\right)$, not $f\left(144\right)$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D is incorrect. This is the value of $f\left(5,184\right)$, not $f\left(144\right)$.
